 # RGE Handoff Document
 
+> **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (ISSUE-345 command-palette recent ordering): task 100 is implemented. `editor-egui-host` now records successful command-palette activations by `Command::diagnostic_id()` into a host-local, in-memory, most-recent-first id list capped at 16. Blank or whitespace-only filters promote currently projected, enabled recent commands first, then append all remaining projected rows in original order; stale ids are ignored, disabled recent rows are not promoted, and task-98 non-blank fuzzy ranking is unchanged. No `editor-shell`, `editor-ui`, plugin runtime/discovery, persistence, second command model, Cargo, workflow, scheduler, architecture-lint, or dispatch automation behavior changed.
+
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (full-automation re-armed for command-palette recent history): task 100 is the next guarded PR-mode automation item. It is a bounded host-local command-palette history slice: record successful palette activations by `Command::diagnostic_id()` into a bounded in-memory most-recent list, use only current projected menu entries, put currently available recent enabled commands first for blank filters, and preserve task-98 non-blank fuzzy ranking. No `editor-shell`, `editor-ui`, plugin runtime/discovery, persistent storage, second command model, Cargo, workflow, scheduler, architecture-lint, or dispatch automation behavior is authorized.
 
 > **Snapshot**: 2026-06-08 (plan-revision gate-context cap): automation hygiene after ISSUE-342 exposed a pre-execution failure path. `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` now caps only the prior executor plan-gate review prose to the last 20,000 characters before feeding it into a Codex plan revision, with an explicit truncation notice. This prevents verbose `needs_changes` gate output from making the next revision prompt exceed Codex's 1,048,576-character input ceiling. +# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-345
+AUTHOR: Executor / Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-08_17-59-14+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs - added host-local recent id storage and records palette activations at the existing enqueue point.
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs - added the recent-list cap/recorder and recent-aware blank-filter ordering.
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs - added focused `command_palette_recent_*` tests.
+- plans/BASELINE.md - added the forward ISSUE-345 recent-ordering baseline section.
+- Status.md - prepended the ISSUE-345 implementation snapshot.
+- HANDOFF.md - prepended the ISSUE-345 implementation snapshot.
+- change.md - appended the ISSUE-345 chronological change and verification entry.
+- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md - marked task 100 done and recorded the implemented cap/behavior.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-345_EXEC_2026-06-08_17-59-14+0300.md - this execution report.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-345_EXEC_2026-06-08_17-59-14+0300.meta.json - finalize sidecar for this execution report.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-345_TASK_2026-06-08_17-35-46+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Re-run
+
+- Claim: the scoped host source had no command-palette recent-history helper,
+  field, or identifier family before execution.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n -E "command_palette_recent|recent_command|recent_commands|RecentCommand|CommandPaletteRecent" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs`.
+  Result: pre-edit re-run returned no matches, exit 1. Post-edit re-run now
+  finds the expected ISSUE-345 implementation at `lib.rs:155`, `lib.rs:306`,
+  `lib.rs:454`, `lib.rs:803`, `lib.rs:894`, `lib.rs:896-897`,
+  `menu.rs:175-183`, `menu.rs:207`, `menu.rs:214`, `menu.rs:234`,
+  `menu.rs:239`, `menu.rs:522`, `menu.rs:551`, and the new
+  `menu_tests.rs:569`, `:602`, `:622`, `:643` tests.
+
+- Claim: the current blank-filter path returned all projected entries before
+  scoring, so ISSUE-345 only changes blank-filter ordering where recent history
+  is supplied.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n "terms.is_empty()\|return entries.iter().collect()\|matches.sort_by_key" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs`.
+  Result: pre-edit re-run matched `menu.rs:187`, `menu.rs:188`, and
+  `menu.rs:202`. Post-edit re-run matches `menu.rs:213` for the blank-filter
+  branch and `menu.rs:228` for non-blank scoring sort; the previous
+  `entries.iter().collect()` blank return is replaced by the recent-aware
+  blank ordering helper.
+
+- Claim: the current non-blank fuzzy behavior is pinned by task 98 / ISSUE-342
+  tests and must remain unchanged by recent ordering.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n "command_palette_filter_fuzzy_matches_label_shortcut_and_command_id\|command_palette_filter_keeps_exact_prefix_and_substring_before_fuzzy\|command_palette_filter_orders_fuzzy_matches_by_compactness" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs`.
+  Result: matches remain present after edits at `menu_tests.rs:694`,
+  `menu_tests.rs:730`, and `menu_tests.rs:755`.
+
+- Claim: palette activation flows through `command_palette_window` returning a
+  command to `lib.rs`, which then enqueues it through the existing
+  menu-command FIFO.
+  Re-run: `git grep -n "command_palette_window\|menu_commands.push(command)" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs`.
+  Result: matches after edits at `lib.rs:154`, `lib.rs:887`, `lib.rs:900`,
+  and `menu.rs:515`. The enqueue path remains the same; ISSUE-345 records the
+  recent id immediately before the existing `menu_commands.push(command)`.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`: added `command_palette_recent_command_ids: Vec<String>` to `EguiHost`, initializes it empty, passes it to the palette window, and records `command.diagnostic_id()` only when the palette returns a command for enqueueing through `MenuCommandHandoff`.
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs`: added `COMMAND_PALETTE_RECENT_COMMAND_LIMIT = 16`, `record_command_palette_recent_command`, and `filter_command_palette_entries_with_recents`. Blank filters now promote enabled projected rows whose diagnostic ids are in the recent list, skip stale ids, avoid duplicate emitted rows, and append all remaining rows in original projection order. Non-blank filtering still uses the existing score/sort path.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs::command_palette_recent_records_most_recent_ids_with_cap_and_deduplication`: proves capped most-recent-first storage, de-duplication by move-to-front, and oldest-drop behavior when recording beyond the cap.
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs::command_palette_recent_blank_filter_promotes_enabled_recent_entries`: proves whitespace filters promote enabled recent commands before the original-order remainder.
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs::command_palette_recent_blank_filter_ignores_stale_ids_and_disabled_promotions`: proves stale ids do not create rows and disabled recent rows stay only in the original-order remainder.
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs::command_palette_recent_non_blank_filter_preserves_fuzzy_score_ordering`: proves recent history does not perturb non-blank exact/prefix/substring/fuzzy ordering.
+
+### Docs

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-345
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-08_17-35-46+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs
+- plans/BASELINE.md
+- Status.md
+- HANDOFF.md
+- change.md
+- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md

+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-345_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-345*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #345 by adding host-local, in-memory recent-command
+ordering to the existing `editor-egui-host` command palette. Palette
+activations should record the activated `Command::diagnostic_id()` in a
+bounded most-recent-first list, and blank command-palette filters should
+promote currently available recent enabled commands ahead of the remaining
+projected entries while preserving the fuzzy non-blank filtering and scoring
+shipped by task 98 / ISSUE-342.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs`
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs`
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs`
+- `plans/BASELINE.md`
+- `Status.md`
+- `HANDOFF.md`
+- `change.md`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`

+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-345_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-345*.md`
+

+- `crates/editor-shell/**`
+- `crates/editor-ui/**`

+- Any source or docs outside the explicit MAY edit list.
+
+### MAY add new files

+- New Rust source files, test files, fixtures, docs, ADRs, schemas, scripts, Cargo entries, crates, architecture lints, workflows, scheduler files, plugin runtime/discovery files, editor-shell files, editor-ui files, persistence/config files, or command-routing artifacts.

+

+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Add a host-local recent-command id list to `EguiHost`, stored only in memory and never persisted. Use a named constant cap of 16 entries unless a more specific existing local palette cap is found.
+- Record only successful command-palette activations: when `command_palette_window` returns an enabled row's `Command` and `lib.rs` enqueues it through the existing `MenuCommandHandoff`, record that command's `diagnostic_id()` string. Do not record palette opens, closes, filter edits, disabled rows, or main-menu activations.
+- Maintain the recent list as most-recent-first. Recording an id already in the list must move that id to the front instead of creating a duplicate, then truncate to the cap.
+- Extend the existing palette projection/filter helper path so a blank or whitespace-only filter returns available recent enabled commands first, in recent-list order, followed by every remaining projected entry in its existing projection order. Recent entries must not appear twice.
+- Ignore stale recent ids that do not match any current projected entry. Disabled recent entries must not be promoted, but they must remain visible in the original-order remainder if they are still projected.
+- Preserve non-blank command-palette behavior from task 98 / ISSUE-342: exact word/field, prefix, substring, fuzzy ordered-subsequence matching, score ordering, multi-term AND semantics, and original-order tie-breaks must remain unchanged by recent history.
+- Keep activation on the existing menu projection and `MenuCommandHandoff` path. Do not add a second command model, second command registry, or new command dispatch path.
+- Add focused tests in `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs` for bounded recent-list de-duplication, stale-id ignoring, blank-filter recent ordering, and unchanged non-blank fuzzy ordering when recent history is present.
+- Update only the forward status/planning records needed to say ISSUE-345 added host-local in-memory recent-command ordering, keeping prior fuzzy-search and remaining non-goal wording intact.
+- Produce this dispatch's EXEC packet with changed files, implementation summary, test/gate results, deviations, and final git status.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The recent-command store contains diagnostic id strings from `Command::diagnostic_id()`, not `Command` values, labels, shortcuts, or persisted records.
+- The recent-command store is host-local memory owned by `editor-egui-host`; it is not serialized, written to disk, moved to `editor-shell`, or exposed as plugin runtime/discovery state.
+- The cap is deterministic and tested. Recording more than the cap leaves exactly the most recent capped set in most-recent-first order.
+- De-duplication is deterministic and tested. Re-activating an existing id moves it to the front without increasing list length.
+- Blank and whitespace-only filters use recent ordering. Current enabled entries whose diagnostic ids appear in the recent list are promoted first, ordered by recent-list position.
+- Stale recent ids are ignored for ordering and never produce missing rows, duplicate rows, panics, or placeholder entries.
+- Disabled entries whose ids are recent are not promoted ahead of enabled rows; they remain present in the unpromoted original-order remainder.
+- All entries not promoted by recency remain in the exact existing projected order produced by `command_palette_entries()`.
+- Non-blank filters ignore recent ordering entirely and keep the task 98 / ISSUE-342 fuzzy score ordering. A recent entry must not jump ahead of a better non-blank fuzzy/exact/prefix/substring match because of history.
+- Existing tests for label/shortcut/diagnostic-id matching, multi-term matching, fuzzy-only matches, exact/prefix/substring outranking fuzzy, fuzzy compactness ordering, keyboard selection, selected-row affordance, disabled-row visibility, and selected command dispatch remain passing or are changed only to accommodate the explicit blank-filter recent-order behavior.
+- `lib.rs` records recents at the palette activation enqueue point only; main-menu clicks continue to enqueue commands without changing recent palette history.
+- `menu.rs` remains the host projection/filtering surface. Do not create new modules or new files for this work.
+- Documentation/task-record updates, if made, are forward-only and limited to ISSUE-345 behavior and verification results.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Required
+
+Every negative or "unchanged" assertion this packet makes about current source
+must list its inverse-search command and result. A negative claim without a
+falsifying search is not a valid premise under Protocol Rule 8.
+
+- Claim: the scoped host source currently has no command-palette recent-history helper, field, or identifier family.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n -E "command_palette_recent|recent_command|recent_commands|RecentCommand|CommandPaletteRecent" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs`
+  Observed result: no matches, exit 1.
+- Claim: the current blank-filter path returns all projected entries before any scoring, so ISSUE-345 is changing blank-filter ordering only where recent history is supplied.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n "terms.is_empty()\|return entries.iter().collect()\|matches.sort_by_key" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs`
+  Observed result: `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs:187` checks `terms.is_empty()`, `menu.rs:188` returns `entries.iter().collect()`, and `menu.rs:202` sorts only the non-blank match list.
+- Claim: the current non-blank fuzzy behavior is already pinned by task 98 / ISSUE-342 tests and must remain unchanged by recent ordering.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n "command_palette_filter_fuzzy_matches_label_shortcut_and_command_id\|command_palette_filter_keeps_exact_prefix_and_substring_before_fuzzy\|command_palette_filter_orders_fuzzy_matches_by_compactness" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs`
+  Observed result: matches at `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs:591`, `menu_tests.rs:627`, and `menu_tests.rs:652`.
+- Claim: palette activation currently flows through `command_palette_window` returning a command to `lib.rs`, which then enqueues it through the existing menu-command FIFO.
+  Falsifying search: `git grep -n "command_palette_window\|menu_commands.push(command)" -- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu.rs`
+  Observed result: `lib.rs:154` imports `command_palette_window`, `lib.rs:882` calls it, `lib.rs:890` enqueues the returned command, and `menu.rs:460` defines the helper.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not persist recents to disk, config, settings, serde state, save data, plugin data, or any external store.
+- Do not add dependencies, Cargo manifest edits, `Cargo.lock` edits, new crates, new modules, new test files, fixtures, schemas, scripts, workflows, architecture lints, or scheduler artifacts.
+- Do not edit `editor-shell`, `editor-ui`, plugin runtime/discovery code, canonical menu definitions, shortcut definitions, menu registry semantics, or accelerator routing.
+- Do not add a second command model, second command registry, command alias system, favorites system, keybinding editor, generalized conflict UI, plugin action execution, or host-shell FIFO replacement.
+- Do not change non-blank command-palette filtering/scoring from task 98 / ISSUE-342 except for mechanical refactoring required to thread recent ids into the blank-filter path.
+- Do not hide disabled rows or change selection/activation semantics except that blank-filter row order may change when enabled recent commands exist.
+- Do not record main-menu activations as palette recents.
+- Do not commit, stage, push, open a PR, edit GitHub labels, post GitHub comments, or rewrite existing dispatch artifacts.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each applicable gate in the
+EXEC packet:
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- `cargo check -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib command_palette_recent -- --nocapture` from the repository root. Expected exit 0 and must include focused tests for bounded de-duplication, stale-id ignoring, blank-filter recent ordering, and unchanged non-blank fuzzy ordering. If exact test names differ, run the exact focused recent-command tests and record the substitution.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-egui-host --lib` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- `git diff --check` from the repository root. Expected exit 0.
+- Final `git status --short --untracked-files=all`, with only allowed ISSUE-345 source/docs/task-record changes and this dispatch's generated artifacts present.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED` and `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI`
+instead of expanding scope if any of the following occur:
+
+- Implementing recent ordering requires editing files outside the MAY edit list or adding files outside the MAY add list.
+- The implementation requires persistence, a second command model, a second command registry, new dependencies, Cargo changes, editor-shell/editor-ui changes, plugin runtime/discovery changes, or command routing changes.
+- Recent ordering cannot be limited to blank or whitespace-only filters without perturbing non-blank fuzzy filtering/scoring.
+- The existing `Command::diagnostic_id()` values cannot uniquely support a deterministic recent list without changing command definitions outside scope.
+- Focused tests cannot be added inside `crates/editor-egui-host/src/menu_tests.rs` without new test files or fixtures.
+- The necessary enqueue/activation site cannot be identified in `crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs` without changing host-shell FIFO behavior.
+- Verification fails for a reason that cannot be fixed within the allowed files.
+- Any tracked file outside the allowed scope is dirty in a way that makes the ISSUE-345 diff ambiguous.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Keep the implementation local and boring. The expected shape is a small
+`Vec<String>` or `VecDeque<String>` field on `EguiHost`, a testable helper in
+`menu.rs` for recording/reordering by diagnostic id, and a `lib.rs` call that
+records the returned palette command immediately before or after pushing it
+through `MenuCommandHandoff`. If helper signatures change, keep the old
+two-argument non-blank filtering behavior available through tests or adapt the
+callers so existing fuzzy tests still prove the same ordering.
+
+For blank filters, think in two passes: first resolve recent ids to current
+enabled projected rows without duplicates, then append every projected row not
+already emitted in original order. This naturally ignores stale ids and keeps
+disabled or non-recent entries visible in the remainder.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is
+expected from this TASK. The only new handoff packet produced by execution
+should be the `ISSUE-345_EXEC_*.md` report plus its sidecar if finalized.
+

+### 2026-06-08 - Command palette recent ordering
+
+**Forward-only follow-up (MENU-COMMAND-PALETTE-RECENT).** Closes the smallest command-history slice for the existing `editor-egui-host` command palette without persisting state, adding a second command model, or changing activation routing. The palette still operates over current menu projection rows and still returns commands for host-side `MenuCommandHandoff` enqueueing.
+
+**Now shipped - host-local recent ordering for blank filters.**
+- `EguiHost` owns an in-memory most-recent-first list of command-palette activation ids, stored as `Command::diagnostic_id()` strings and capped at 16.
+- Successful command-palette activations record the id at the existing palette return -> `MenuCommandHandoff` enqueue point. Main-menu activations do not update palette recents.
+- Re-recording an existing id moves it to the front without duplication, then the list is truncated to the cap.
+- Blank or whitespace-only filters promote currently projected, enabled recent commands first, in recent-list order, then append every remaining projected row in original order.
+- Stale recent ids are ignored. Disabled recent rows are not promoted, but remain visible in the original-order remainder when still projected.
+- Non-blank filters ignore recency and keep the task-98 exact word/field, prefix, substring, fuzzy ordered-subsequence score ordering and original-order tie-breaks.
+- Host tests pin bounded de-duplication, stale-id ignoring, blank-filter recent ordering, disabled-row remainder behavior, and unchanged non-blank fuzzy ordering with recents present.
+
+**Still open - explicitly NOT closed here:** persistent command history/favorites, a second command model, plugin runtime/action execution beyond FIFO enqueue, host->shell FIFO replacement, keybinding editor, generalized conflict-resolution UI, Cargo, scheduler, architecture-lint rule/config behavior, dispatch automation behavior, and task arming.
+
+**Scope:** `editor-egui-host` palette state/filter helpers/tests plus top-level status docs and task-list bookkeeping; no `editor-ui`, no `editor-shell`, no plugin runtime, no persistence, no Cargo/workflow/scheduler/automation behavior.
+
 ### 2026-06-08 - Command palette fuzzy matching
 
 **Forward-only follow-up (MENU-COMMAND-PALETTE-FUZZY).** Closes the command-palette fuzzy matching/scoring gap in the existing `editor-egui-host` palette filter without inventing a second command model or changing activation routing. The palette still filters the already-projected menu rows and still returns commands for host-side `MenuCommandHandoff` enqueueing.
